title: VibeLoc (NeighbourSwipe) — Mobile-first swipe-based neighbourhood matching MVP

requirements.text: Create a mobile-first React UI shell for the app named “VibeLoc” with the product label “NeighbourSwipe”, including top-level navigation and routing for: Onboarding, Discover (swipe feed), Shortlist (liked neighbourhoods), and Profile/Preferences.

quotes: Build a mobile-first MVP application called (VibeLoc): “NeighbourSwipe” — a swipe-based neighbourhood discovery and matching platform for urban renters in Kenya.

acceptanceCriteria: App is usable on mobile viewport widths (e.g., 360px) without horizontal scrolling.
There are distinct screens/routes for Onboarding, Discover, Shortlist, and Profile/Preferences.
User-facing text is in English and consistently uses the names “VibeLoc” and “NeighbourSwipe”.

requirements.text: Implement onboarding to capture renter preferences used for matching: monthly budget range (KES), commute preferences (primary area/direction + max commute time), and lifestyle preferences (selectable tags). Persist onboarding completion state and the captured preferences so the user can return later and edit them.

quotes: The app helps users match with neighbourhoods based on lifestyle, commute, and budget — before they ever see specific houses.

acceptanceCriteria: Onboarding collects: budget range in KES, commute preference(s), and lifestyle tags.
After completing onboarding, the user is taken to the Discover swipe feed.
Preferences can be viewed and edited in Profile/Preferences and changes affect subsequent recommendations.
Preferences persist across reloads for the same signed-in user (or same browser session if not signed in).

requirements.text: Create a backend neighbourhood catalog for Nairobi, Kenya with an initial curated set of neighbourhood entries (seed data) including: name, short description, typical rent band (KES), commute notes, lifestyle tags, and 1 image reference per neighbourhood (static asset filename).

quotes: VibeLoc is a swipe-based neighbourhood discovery app for urban renters and gig-economy workers in Nairobi, Kenya.

acceptanceCriteria: Backend exposes a method to fetch neighbourhoods (seeded) with the required fields.
At least 10 Nairobi neighbourhoods are available in the catalog for the MVP.
Each neighbourhood includes tags and a rent band that can be used for matching.

requirements.text: Implement matching/recommendation logic that ranks neighbourhoods for a user based on their saved preferences (budget fit, commute fit, lifestyle tag overlap). Return a scored, ordered list for the Discover feed.

quotes: The app helps users match with neighbourhoods based on lifestyle, commute, and budget.

acceptanceCriteria: Backend returns neighbourhoods ordered by a computed match score for the current user.
The ranking reflects changes when the user updates budget/commute/lifestyle preferences.
Already-swiped neighbourhoods (liked or disliked) are excluded from the Discover results.

requirements.text: Build the Discover swipe experience similar to Tinder: show one neighbourhood card at a time with image, name, key highlights (rent band, commute note, top tags), and clear swipe actions (Like / Nope) supporting both gestures and buttons.

quotes: The experience should feel similar to Tinder, but users swipe on neighbourhoods instead of people.

acceptanceCriteria: User can Like/Nope via swipe gestures on mobile and via explicit buttons.
After a decision, the next card appears with minimal latency.
Card displays: image, name, rent band in KES, a short description, and tags.
If the feed is exhausted, show an empty state with a call-to-action to adjust preferences or reset swipes.

requirements.text: Persist swipe decisions (like/dislike) per user and implement a “Shortlist” screen that lists liked neighbourhoods with basic details and the ability to remove (unlike) an item.

quotes: Saved/liked neighbourhoods list (shortlist) with basic details for revisit

acceptanceCriteria: Liking a neighbourhood adds it to the Shortlist.
Shortlist shows a list of liked neighbourhoods with image thumbnail, name, and key highlights.
User can remove an item from the Shortlist, and it no longer appears there.
Likes persist across reloads for the same signed-in user (or same browser session if not signed in).

requirements.text: Add Internet Identity sign-in support so that preferences and likes can be stored per Principal on the backend; provide a clear signed-in/signed-out state in the UI without editing immutable auth hook files.

quotes: Build a mobile-first MVP application called (VibeLoc): “NeighbourSwipe”

acceptanceCriteria: User can sign in and sign out using Internet Identity.
UI indicates whether the user is signed in (e.g., in Profile/Preferences).
When signed in, likes and preferences are fetched from and saved to the backend for that Principal.
No changes are requested to files under the immutable paths listed in system constraints.

requirements.text: Define and apply a coherent visual theme suitable for an urban Kenya/Nairobi renter audience (mobile-first), including color palette, typography, and component styling, and use it consistently across all screens.

quotes: Build a mobile-first MVP application called (VibeLoc): “NeighbourSwipe”

acceptanceCriteria: Theme is consistent across Onboarding, Discover, Shortlist, and Profile/Preferences.
Design avoids a default “template look” and uses a distinct style (do not use a blue/purple theme).
Interactive elements (buttons, swipe affordances) are visually clear on mobile.

requirements.text: Integrate generated static images as frontend assets and use them in the UI (logo in header/onboarding; neighbourhood placeholder imagery in cards where applicable), served directly from the frontend (not via backend).

quotes: swipe-based neighbourhood discovery and matching platform

acceptanceCriteria: Images are stored under frontend/public/assets/generated and referenced by the UI.
Logo is visible on at least the onboarding screen and/or app header.
Neighbourhood cards display an image (neighbourhood-specific if available, otherwise a consistent placeholder).

constraints: Use English for all user-facing text.
Backend must remain a single Motoko actor (all logic in backend/main.mo).
Do not edit any frontend files listed as immutable paths in SYSTEM_CONTEXT; compose them from other components instead.
Mobile-first UI: primary layout and interactions must work well on touch devices.
nonGoals: Do not implement listing or browsing of specific houses/units (neighbourhoods only).
Do not implement maps, geolocation, or GPS-based features.
Do not implement chat, real-time messaging, push notifications, or email/SMS.
Do not implement payments or subscription billing.

required: App logo mark + wordmark in a modern, warm, Nairobi-inspired style (vibeloc-logo.dim_512x512.png)
Neighbourhood card placeholder illustration with subtle city/urban motif, warm tones (neighbourhood-placeholder.dim_1080x1350.png)
Onboarding background pattern/illustration with abstract urban shapes, low-contrast (onboarding-background.dim_1080x1920.png)
